description_long: This object consists of two upright, purple tulip flowers with elongated, cup-shaped blooms. The petals are smooth, overlapping, and display subtle gradients of lavender, violet, and hints of white, giving a natural, delicate appearance. Each flower is supported by a slender, green stem that rises vertically from the base. Several long, narrow, lanceolate leaves emerge from the lower part of the stems, adding a fresh green contrast to the purple blooms. The flowers are closely paired, with their stems and leaves slightly intertwined, suggesting they are part of the same plant or bouquet. The overall form is elegant and typical of tulips often found in urban parks, gardens, or decorative planters.
description: A pair of upright, purple tulip flowers with smooth, overlapping petals and slender green stems, accompanied by long, narrow leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing both tulip blooms fully visible, with overlapping petals and green leaves at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the blooms and the close proximity of the two flowers, with one flower partially obscuring the other.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with a slight shift in leaf arrangement and petal overlap, both blooms still clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the other profile of the flowers, with petals overlapping and leaves visible at the base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, overlapping whorl typical of tulip flowers, forming a cup-like structure with smooth, continuous edges and moderate symmetry.
